pyf001.title: Can computers think?
pyf001.description: Understand that computers follow instructions and the Input - Process - Output model.
objectives: Understand that programming means giving clear instructions
Separate Input, Process and Output
Recognize programming in daily life

blocks.title: Computers do not guess
blocks.content: Computers are fast, but they do not understand human intention automatically. A program describes clear steps for the computer to follow.

blocks.title: Input - Process - Output
blocks.content: Input is data provided to a program. Process is the handling step. Output is the result. Example: enter 2 and 3, process addition, output 5.

blocks.title: Analyze a daily activity
blocks.content: Choose an activity such as making noodles or opening a learning app. Write its Input, Process and Output.

blocks.title: How to study this lesson
blocks.content: The focus is to describe Input - Process - Output for a process. The learner should move from a small example to a checked practice task.
steps: Read the objectives before coding so the target is clear.
Run an example related to Input - Process - Output and observe the output.
Change one small value in the example and see how the program responds.
Complete the practice task, then compare with the checklist or test cases.
Record the error and the fix for later lessons.

blocks.title: Common mistakes to avoid
blocks.content: The most common mistake is confusing Process with Output. When an error appears, do not change code randomly; read the message, check input, inspect intermediate values, then fix it.
items: confusing Process with Output
Not testing small parts before combining them into the full program
Reading code without checking the actual output

blocks.title: Checkpoint before the next lesson
blocks.content: The learner should meet these signs before moving forward.
items: Explain what Input - Process - Output is used for
Run the example again without looking at the full solution
Score at least 70% on the quick quiz
If the lesson has a code test, pass all sample test cases

quiz.question: After "Can computers think?", what is the most important learning goal?

options.A: describe Input - Process - Output for a process
options.B: Only remember the interface color

quiz.explain: This lesson focuses on the ability to describe Input - Process - Output for a process.

quiz.question: Which study approach best fits a time-limited lesson?

options.B: Read the concept, run a small example, then modify one requirement

quiz.explain: A good flow is concept, observed example, and guided independent practice.

quiz.question: What is the central concept/tool in this lesson?

options.B: Project folder name

quiz.explain: Input - Process - Output is the main idea to master in this lesson.

quiz.question: When a program gives a wrong result, what is the best action?

options.B: Randomly change many lines at once
options.D: Check input, each processing step, and output

quiz.explain: Logical debugging means checking each part to locate the real issue.

quiz.question: Which mistake is common in this lesson?

options.B: The computer fixes every error automatically
options.C: There is no need to run tests

quiz.explain: The mistake to watch for is confusing Process with Output.
